name: Jávea – Circular walk along the Balcón del Mar to Cala Granadella
name_original: Jávea – Paseo circular por el Balcón del Mar hasta la Cala Granadella
region_intro: Touring cycling around Jávea/Xàbia offers diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths to challenging mountain trails. The region features dramatic headlands like Cap de la Nau and Cap de Sant Antoni, alongside the Montgó Natural Park with its steep ascents. Inland, the Jalón Valley provides rolling roads through vineyards, while Granadella Forest Park offers routes through dense pine forests. Best touring cycling routes around Jávea/Xàbia The most popular touring cycling route is Jávea – Circular walk along the Balcón del Mar to Cala Granadella, a 20.4 miles (32.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive coastal views along the Balcón del Mar. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Port of Moraira – Punta de l'Arenal Viewpoint loop from Trenecito, a difficult 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the coastline and passes by the Punta de l'Arenal Viewpoint. Local touring cyclists also love the Old Quarry Benissa Coast – Gorgos River Trail loop from Trenecito, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Gorgos River area, often completed in about 3 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Jávea/Xàbia is defined by coastal paths, dramatic headlands, and routes through pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Jávea/Xàbia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 360 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Jávea/Xàbia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Jávea/Xàbia offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 110 options available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Jávea/Xàbia boasts a favorable climate that allows for year-round touring cycling. The mild weather ensures comfortable riding conditions, making it an ideal destination for cyclists throughout the seasons.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Yes, Jávea/Xàbia offers several easy and family-friendly options. Coastal paths, such as the Port-Cala Blanca Family Route, are generally flat and ideal for relaxed rides. The "Vía Verde" (Greenway) between Dénia and Gandia also provides a flat, pleasant stage through orange groves, suitable for all ages.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: The region offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You can expect stunning Mediterranean coastal views, dramatic headlands like Cap de la Nau and Cap de Sant Antoni, dense pine forests in areas like Granadella Forest Park, and rolling roads through vineyards in the inland Jalón Valley. The iconic Montgó Massif also provides challenging routes with panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Yes, many routes in Jávea/Xàbia are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952000884/javea-circular-walk-along-the-balcon-del-mar-to-cala-granadella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jávea – Circular walk along the Balcón del Mar to Cala Granadella&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive coastal views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20478869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Moraira – Punta de l'Arenal Viewpoint loop from Trenecito&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic views of the coastline.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Jávea/Xàbia is rich in natural beauty and viewpoints. You can cycle past the dramatic cliffs of Cap de Sant Antoni, explore trails within Montgó Natural Park, or enjoy the stunning vistas from the Route of the Miradores. Specific highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2211732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgó Summit&lt;/a&gt;, which offer breathtaking views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, touring cycling routes often pass through or near historical landmarks. You can discover ancient caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic Old Town of Jávea with its buildings like the Church of St. Bartholomew, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Aixa Ruins and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Jávea/Xàbia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 360 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, from stunning coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the breathtaking panoramic views offered throughout the region.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Jávea/Xàbia is well-known for its challenging routes, particularly those in the Montgó Natural Park with steep ascents and rocky paths. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38247066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Quarry Benissa Coast – Gorgos River Trail loop from Trenecito&lt;/a&gt; also feature varied and demanding terrain, making them suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Jávea/Xàbia pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants. The region's popularity with cyclists means there are often cyclist-friendly establishments, especially in coastal areas and larger towns, perfect for a break and refreshment.

Q: Is it possible to combine touring cycling with other outdoor activities in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Yes, Jávea/Xàbia is an excellent base for combining touring cycling with other outdoor pursuits. The region offers extensive hiking trails in Montgó Natural Park and Granadella Forest Park, as well as water activities like kayaking, snorkeling, and paddleboarding in its crystal-clear waters and hidden coves.

Q: Are there specific routes that offer extensive coastal views?
A: Many routes in Jávea/Xàbia provide spectacular coastal views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952000884/javea-circular-walk-along-the-balcon-del-mar-to-cala-granadella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jávea – Circular walk along the Balcón del Mar to Cala Granadella&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its extensive coastal vistas. Additionally, the route from Arenal Beach to Cabo de la Nao offers gentle gradients and refreshing sea breezes along the coast.
tips: The Caletes viewpoint near Jávea is a picturesque place that offers spectacular views of the Mediterranean Sea and the rocky coastal landscape. The intense blue of the Mediterranean meets the white and ochre-colored rocks of the cliffs and the green of the Mediterranean vegetation. A truly impressive play of colors, especially at sunrise or sunset. The Mirador is a perfect place to enjoy the beauty of the Jávea coast in peace and quiet. A great destination for a tour! You should definitely plan it if you are cycling in the region. 🚴‍♀️

A very beautiful vantage point. Although not located directly on the (steep) coast, it is easily accessible from the paved CV-742 road. The small rocky island "L'illa del Portitxol" located off the coast is particularly well-preserved from here.

A beautiful view over the coast of Xàbia (Valencian / Catalan) or Javea (Spanish) and the islands and rocks in front of it. Fascinating! 

As is often the case on the Costa Blanca, the afternoon is the right time for a beautiful view and photos at this viewpoint, as you then have the sun at your back. Otherwise you are looking into the sun and the photos look "slightly shady". 😉

Cabo de la Nao, the easternmost point of the province of Alicante, is a spectacular cape on the Costa Blanca. Its steep limestone cliffs offer breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea – on clear days, the view extends as far as the Balearic islands of Ibiza and Formentera. The cape separates the bays of Alicante and Valencia and is surrounded by hidden coves and small islands.

A striking landmark is the white lighthouse, built in 1928, which stands 120 meters above the sea and shines its light up to 23 nautical miles away. A visit is worthwhile not only for the impressive landscape but also for the picturesque viewpoints that offer unforgettable panoramic views of the deep blue Mediterranean.

A cool location, well worth the drive or a detour during your tour! Cala Granadella is a beautiful little cove near Jávea (Xàbia). The cove, with its crystal-clear, turquoise waters, is surrounded by impressive cliffs and Mediterranean vegetation, offering a breathtaking natural backdrop. The beach is made of small pebbles instead of sand. It can get very crowded during peak season, so it's worth arriving early. Nearby are restaurants serving fresh seafood and Spanish specialties.
Stone cross at the junction of Camí Vell de Teulada and Carretera de Xàbia a Benitatxell. Also known as the Cruz de Tarraula.

waypoints.name: View of Portitxol Island from the Creu del Portitxol
